title: Dimeria lehmannii
taxonomy: Dimeria lehmannii is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Dimeria, which is a group of plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Dimeria lehmannii is a member of the subfamily Panicoideae, a large group within the Poaceae family that includes many grass species. The Poaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 12,000 known species. Dimeria lehmannii's classification reflects its botanical relationship to other grasses and its characteristics as a tropical grass species.
uses: Dimeria lehmannii has been found to have potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is often used to treat various health issues, such as fever and skin problems. Further studies are needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits to human health.
